---
title: "nothing"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)


############################################################
# MODEL SETTINGS
############################################################

m <- 57
hours <- 24

v_min <- 2^(-5)
delta_v <- 0.125

size_index <- 1:m

v_i <- v_min * 2^((size_index - 1) * delta_v)


############################################################
# TRUE PARAMETERS
############################################################

gamma_max_true <- 0.20
delta_max_true <- 0.03
E_star_true <- 500
a_true <- 2
b_true <- 2

# Kernel memory-decay parameter
p_true <- 0.40

# tau_max is fixed, not estimated
tau_max <- 33

true_params <- c(
  gamma_max = gamma_max_true,
  delta_max = delta_max_true,
  E_star = E_star_true,
  a = a_true,
  b = b_true,
  p = p_true
)


############################################################
# PERIODIC LIGHT FUNCTION
############################################################

E_t <- function(t) {
  
  # Keeps time within a repeating 24-hour cycle
  t_mod <- t %% 24
  
  E <- 1000 * max(
    0,
    sin(2 * pi * t_mod / 24)
  )
  
  return(E)
}


# Plot instantaneous light

time_grid <- seq(0, 48, by = 0.1)

light_values <- sapply(
  time_grid,
  E_t
)

plot(
  time_grid,
  light_values,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  xlab = "Hours",
  ylab = "E(t)",
  main = "Instantaneous Light"
)


############################################################
# GEOMETRIC KERNEL WEIGHTS
############################################################

kernel_weights <- function(p, tau_max) {
  
  # Prevent p from becoming exactly 0 or 1
  p <- min(
    max(p, 1e-8),
    1 - 1e-8
  )
  
  tau_values <- 0:tau_max
  
  weights <- p * (1 - p)^tau_values
  
  # Normalize so all weights add to 1
  weights <- weights / sum(weights)
  
  return(weights)
}


############################################################
# EFFECTIVE LIGHT CHI(t)
############################################################

E_kernel <- function(t, p, tau_max) {
  
  tau_values <- 0:tau_max
  
  weights <- kernel_weights(
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  light_values <- sapply(
    tau_values,
    function(tau) {
      E_t(t - tau)
    }
  )
  
  chi <- sum(
    weights * light_values
  )
  
  return(chi)
}


############################################################
# KERNEL-DEPENDENT GROWTH FUNCTION
############################################################

gamma_t <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    E_star,
    p,
    tau_max
) {
  
  chi_t <- E_kernel(
    t = t,
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  gamma <- gamma_max *
    (1 - exp(-chi_t / E_star))
  
  return(gamma)
}


############################################################
# DIVISION FUNCTION
############################################################

delta_i_t <- function(t, delta_max, a, b) {
  
  # Division begins at hour 6
  if (t < 6) {
    return(rep(0, m))
  }
  
  delta_i <- delta_max *
    ((a * v_i)^b /
       (1 + (a * v_i)^b))
  
  return(delta_i)
}


############################################################
# MATRIX A(t)
############################################################

matrix_A <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    delta_max,
    E_star,
    a,
    b,
    p,
    tau_max
) {
  
  A <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = m,
    ncol = m
  )
  
  # Growth depends on kernel-weighted light
  gamma <- gamma_t(
    t = t,
    gamma_max = gamma_max,
    E_star = E_star,
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  delta_i <- delta_i_t(
    t = t,
    delta_max = delta_max,
    a = a,
    b = b
  )
  
  # Number of size classes corresponding to doubling
  j <- as.integer(
    1 + 1 / delta_v
  )
  
  
  ##########################################################
  # GROWTH
  ##########################################################
  
  # Cells move to the next larger size class
  
  for (i in 1:(m - 1)) {
    
    A[i + 1, i] <-
      A[i + 1, i] +
      gamma * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  
  
  # Largest cells stay in the largest class
  # if they grow without dividing
  
  A[m, m] <-
    A[m, m] +
    gamma * (1 - delta_i[m])
  
  
  ##########################################################
  # DIVISION
  ##########################################################
  
  # Small cells that divide produce daughters in class 1
  
  for (i in 2:(j - 1)) {
    
    A[1, i] <-
      A[1, i] +
      2 * delta_i[i]
  }
  
  
  # Larger cells produce daughters approximately
  # half the parent's volume
  
  for (i in j:m) {
    
    smaller_class <- i + 1 - j
    
    A[smaller_class, i] <-
      A[smaller_class, i] +
      2 * delta_i[i]
  }
  
  
  ##########################################################
  # STASIS
  ##########################################################
  
  # Class 1 includes cells that stay and daughters
  # produced by division of class-1 cells
  
  A[1, 1] <-
    A[1, 1] +
    ((1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[1])) +
    2 * delta_i[1]
  
  
  for (i in 2:(m - 1)) {
    
    A[i, i] <-
      A[i, i] +
      (1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  
  
  # Largest size class
  
  A[m, m] <-
    A[m, m] +
    (1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[m])
  
  
  return(A)
}


############################################################
# MATRIX B(t)
#
# One hour consists of six ten-minute A matrices
############################################################

matrix_B <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    delta_max,
    E_star,
    a,
    b,
    p,
    tau_max,
    dt = 1 / 6
) {
  
  # Start with the 57 x 57 identity matrix
  B <- diag(m)
  
  n_steps <- as.integer(
    round(1 / dt)
  )
  
  for (k in 0:(n_steps - 1)) {
    
    time_now <- t + k * dt
    
    A_k <- matrix_A(
      t = time_now,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
    # After the loop:
    # B = A_5 %*% A_4 %*% ... %*% A_0
    
    B <- A_k %*% B
  }
  
  return(B)
}


############################################################
# INITIAL SIZE DISTRIBUTION w(0)
############################################################

w0 <- dnorm(
  size_index,
  mean = 20,
  sd = 6
)

w0 <- w0 / sum(w0)

plot(
  v_i,
  w0,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  xlab = "Cell volume",
  ylab = "Initial proportion",
  main = "Initial Size Distribution"
)


############################################################
# TRUE KERNEL MODEL DISTRIBUTION
############################################################

W_true <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = hours,
  ncol = m
)

# First row represents hour 0
W_true[1, ] <- w0

for (row in 1:(hours - 1)) {
  
  # Row 1 corresponds to time 0
  time_start <- row - 1
  
  B_t <- matrix_B(
    t = time_start,
    gamma_max = gamma_max_true,
    delta_max = delta_max_true,
    E_star = E_star_true,
    a = a_true,
    b = b_true,
    p = p_true,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  W_true[row + 1, ] <-
    B_t %*% W_true[row, ]
  
  W_true[row + 1, ] <-
    W_true[row + 1, ] /
    sum(W_true[row + 1, ])
}


# Every row should sum to approximately 1

print(rowSums(W_true))


############################################################
# CREATE FAKE OBSERVATIONS
############################################################

set.seed(123)

N <- 1000000

Counts <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = hours,
  ncol = m
)

for (t in 1:hours) {
  
  Counts[t, ] <- rmultinom(
    n = 1,
    size = N,
    prob = W_true[t, ]
  )
}

W_obs <- Counts / rowSums(Counts)


############################################################
# MODEL FOR GUESSED PARAMETERS
############################################################

guess_model <- function(params, W) {
  
  # params =
  # c(gamma_max, delta_max, E_star, a, b, p)
  
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]
  p <- params[6]
  
  W_pred <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = hours,
    ncol = m
  )
  
  # Only the initial observed distribution is used
  W_pred[1, ] <- W[1, ]
  
  for (row in 1:(hours - 1)) {
    
    time_start <- row - 1
    
    B_t <- matrix_B(
      t = time_start,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
    # Predict from the previous predicted distribution
    W_pred[row + 1, ] <-
      B_t %*% W_pred[row, ]
    
    W_pred[row + 1, ] <-
      W_pred[row + 1, ] /
      sum(W_pred[row + 1, ])
  }
  
  return(W_pred)
}


############################################################
# OBJECTIVE FUNCTION
############################################################

objective_function <- function(params, W) {
  
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]
  p <- params[6]
  
  
  # Parameter checks
  
  if (
    gamma_max < 0 ||
    gamma_max > 0.5 ||
    delta_max < 0 ||
    delta_max > 0.15 ||
    E_star <= 0 ||
    a <= 0 ||
    b <= 0 ||
    p <= 0 ||
    p >= 1
  ) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  W_pred <- guess_model(
    params = params,
    W = W
  )
  
  
  # Difference between observed and predicted distributions
  
  error <- sum(
    (W - W_pred)^2
  )
  
  return(error)
}


############################################################
# FIT THE SIX PARAMETERS
############################################################

initial_guess <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.20,
  delta_max = 0.03,
  E_star = 500,
  a = 2,
  b = 2,
  p = 0.40
)

fit <- optim(
  par = initial_guess,
  fn = objective_function,
  W = W_obs,
  method = "L-BFGS-B",
  
  lower = c(
    gamma_max = 0.001,
    delta_max = 0.001,
    E_star = 100,
    a = 0.1,
    b = 0.1,
    p = 0.001
  ),
  
  upper = c(
    gamma_max = 0.5,
    delta_max = 0.15,
    E_star = 5000,
    a = 30,
    b = 10,
    p = 0.999
  ),
  
  control = list(
    maxit = 1000
  )
)


estimated_params <- fit$par


############################################################
# PARAMETER RESULTS
############################################################

results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c(
    "gamma_max",
    "delta_max",
    "E_star",
    "a",
    "b",
    "p"
  ),
  
  True = c(
    gamma_max_true,
    delta_max_true,
    E_star_true,
    a_true,
    b_true,
    p_true
  ),
  
  Estimated = estimated_params
)

results$Percent_Error <- 100 *
  (results$Estimated - results$True) /
  results$True

print(results)

cat(
  "\nOptimization convergence:",
  fit$convergence,
  "\n"
)

cat(
  "Objective function value:",
  fit$value,
  "\n"
)


############################################################
# FITTED DISTRIBUTION
############################################################

W_fit <- guess_model(
  params = estimated_params,
  W = W_obs
)


############################################################
# HEATMAP FUNCTION
############################################################

plot_heatmap <- function(
    W,
    title_name,
    maximum_proportion
) {
  
  df <- melt(W)
  
  colnames(df) <- c(
    "Hour",
    "SizeClass",
    "Proportion"
  )
  
  # Matrix row 1 represents hour 0
  df$Hour <- df$Hour - 1
  
  # Convert size-class number into cell volume
  df$CellVolume <- v_i[df$SizeClass]
  
  ggplot(
    df,
    aes(
      x = CellVolume,
      y = Hour,
      fill = Proportion
    )
  ) +
    geom_tile() +
    
    scale_x_log10() +
    
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = seq(0, 23, by = 3)
    ) +
    
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = c(
        "blue",
        "cyan",
        "yellow",
        "red"
      ),
      limits = c(0, maximum_proportion)
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = title_name,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Kernel model: p = ",
        round(p_true, 2),
        ", tau_max = ",
        tau_max
      ),
      x = expression(
        "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
      ),
      y = "Hour after dawn",
      fill = "Proportion"
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold"
      )
    )
}


############################################################
# PLOT THE THREE HEATMAPS
############################################################

# Same color scale for direct comparison

common_max <- max(
  W_true,
  W_obs,
  W_fit
)

p_true_plot <- plot_heatmap(
  W = W_true,
  title_name = "True Kernel Model Distribution",
  maximum_proportion = common_max
)

p_obs_plot <- plot_heatmap(
  W = W_obs,
  title_name = "Fake Observed Distribution",
  maximum_proportion = common_max
)

p_fit_plot <- plot_heatmap(
  W = W_fit,
  title_name = "Fitted Kernel Model Distribution",
  maximum_proportion = common_max
)

print(p_true_plot)
print(p_obs_plot)
print(p_fit_plot)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
